deep_review.name: Deep Review
deep_review.trigger: Any review, audit, or analysis task
deep_review.description: Long-context aware review. Read ALL files first, never comment before complete read.
steps: Build mental model of full system

architecture.name: Architecture Creation
architecture.trigger: System design, new component, greenfield project
architecture.description: Requirements → ADRs → Component diagram → Data flow → Risk register
anti_patterns: Starting implementation before architecture is agreed

phase_plan.name: Phase Planning
phase_plan.trigger: Any project or feature requiring multiple implementation steps
phase_plan.description: Break work into phases with milestones, dependencies, exit criteria

todo_plan.name: Step-by-Step TODO Planning
todo_plan.description: Atomic, ordered, verifiable TODO list. Each TODO has: action, file, acceptance criterion, tier.
anti_patterns: TODOs that span multiple files without a plan

coding.name: Standard Coding
coding.trigger: Single-file or simple multi-file coding task
coding.description: Read → Plan → Implement → Verify. Never code before reading.
steps: State what you will change and why
anti_patterns: Coding from memory without reading current file
Not verifying edit was applied correctly

complex_coding.name: Complex Coding
complex_coding.trigger: Multi-file feature, refactor, or algorithm implementation
complex_coding.description: Full protocol: scope → deps → implement in order → cross-check → qa
anti_patterns: Skipping dependency analysis for multi-file changes

multi_file_edit.name: Multi-File Edit
multi_file_edit.description: Plan edit order → Read all → Edit one at a time → Verify each → Integration check
anti_patterns: Editing multiple files without a plan
Not verifying each file after edit

e2e_integration.name: E2E Integration
e2e_integration.trigger: Wiring services together, API contracts, cross-system integration
e2e_integration.description: Define contracts → Implement → Verify each point → Test failure paths
steps: Implement with timeouts and error handling
Test graceful failure of each point

qa.name: Detailed QA
qa.trigger: Before any commit, PR, or release
qa.description: Structured QA covering: syntax, tests, security, integration, performance
steps: Fix all CRITICAL findings before proceeding
Fix all HIGH findings before release
Document MEDIUM and LOW as known issues
anti_patterns: Declaring PASS with open CRITICAL findings

rca.name: Intelligent RCA
rca.trigger: Any error, failure, or unexpected behaviour
rca.description: Evidence-based root cause analysis. No hypotheses without code evidence.
steps: Verify each hypothesis against actual code
anti_patterns: Fixing the symptom not the root cause
Stating root cause before reading code

bug_fix.name: Intelligent Bug Fix
bug_fix.trigger: Any bug report or unexpected behaviour
bug_fix.description: Reproduce → Locate → Root cause → Minimal fix → Verify → Document
anti_patterns: Not documenting the fix for future reference

implementation.name: Intelligent Implementation
implementation.trigger: Any feature or system implementation task
implementation.description: Full lifecycle: scope → architecture → plan → implement → qa → memory

agentic.name: Intelligent Agentic
agentic.trigger: Complex autonomous task requiring multiple steps and tools
agentic.description: Goal → Decompose → Orchestrate subagents → Verify → Memory
anti_patterns: Not verifying subagent output before using it

subagent.name: Intelligent Subagent
subagent.trigger: When Claude is operating as a subagent within a larger orchestration
subagent.description: Bounded execution: only declared tools, only declared scope, structured output only
steps: Return structured output — do not apply changes autonomously
Escalate if scope needs to expand
anti_patterns: Using tools not in declared allowed list
